name: assumption-testing-agent-pack
description: Machine-readable schemas and rules for assumption-test strategy generation.

evidenceStrengthScale.description: Scale from 1 (softest/most qualitative) to 5 (hardest/most behavioral). Reflects how directly the method captures real-world behavior vs. self-reported opinion.

values.1: Soft signal - qualitative, self-reported, or highly contextual. Useful for orientation and hypothesis generation.
values.2: Weak-to-moderate signal - provides directional evidence but limited confidence on its own.
values.3: Moderate signal - meaningful evidence; enough to act on when combined with another method.
values.4: Strong signal - direct behavioral or high-fidelity evidence; high confidence when ICP-filtered.
values.5: Hard signal - statistically significant behavioral evidence at scale; sufficient for major investment decisions.

evidenceStrengthScale.note: Methods with string values like '2-5' or '4-5' indicate that strength varies by execution quality and traffic volume.
